findings.evidence: `rg "IsJHolomorphic.*comp" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphic.lean` finds only lines 193 and 221; Mathlib provides `HasFDerivWithinAt.comp` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/FDeriv/Comp.lean:74-76`.
findings.fix: Add `IsJHolomorphicWithinAt.comp` with a `MapsTo f s t` or `Tendsto f (𝓝[s] x) (𝓝[t] (f x))` hypothesis, and `IsJHolomorphicOn.comp` with `MapsTo f s t`, using `HasFDerivWithinAt.comp`.
findings.issue: `IsJHolomorphicWithinAt` and `IsJHolomorphicOn` are public, but composition is only exposed for `IsJHolomorphicAt` and global `IsJHolomorphic`, leaving local/setwise users without the operation lemma for the public API.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "\@\[simp\]|isJHolomorphic.*const|isJHolomorphic.*id|IsComplexLinearMap" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphic.lean` shows only `isJHolomorphicOn_univ` is tagged `@[simp]`, while the normal-form lemmas are at lines 53, 60, 184, 189, 214, and 218.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `isComplexLinearMap_zero`, `isComplexLinearMap_id`, `isJHolomorphicAt_const`, `isJHolomorphicAt_id`, `isJHolomorphic_const`, and `isJHolomorphic_id`; add within/on variants too if those are intended normal forms.
findings.issue: The zero/id/constant holomorphic normal forms are unannotated, so `simp` will not close the basic goals introduced by these public definitions.

summary: The public predicates expose within/setwise holomorphicity but do not provide the corresponding composition API, and the basic normal-form lemmas are not marked for automation. Downstream local chart proofs would have to unfold the new predicates or reprove derivative composition and terminal cases.
